course.title: Functional Programming in Modern Python
course.description: Python developers ready to design transformation-heavy systems with explicit effects, reusable callables, lazy data flow, and law-oriented tests. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate functional problem decomposition and explicit effect boundaries through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate pure transformations, immutable updates, and function composition through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate iterators, generators, itertools, functools, closures, and decorators through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate result modeling, pattern matching, property tests, and functional-core architecture through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Distinguish value transformations from identity-changing commands in an execution trace.
misconceptions: Functional programming means banning every object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish value transformations from identity-changing commands in an execution trace.

competencies.statement: Locate I/O, mutation, time, randomness, and exceptions and move them behind explicit boundaries.
misconceptions: A function is pure whenever it has a return statement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Locate I/O, mutation, time, randomness, and exceptions and move them behind explicit boundaries.

competencies.statement: Test whether an expression can be replaced by its value without changing program behavior.
misconceptions: Same-looking outputs prove referential transparency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test whether an expression can be replaced by its value without changing program behavior.

competencies.statement: Trace expression evaluation without relying on hidden statement order.
misconceptions: Expressions and statements have identical substitution rules.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace expression evaluation without relying on hidden statement order.

competencies.statement: Choose functional, imperative, or mixed structure from change risk and evidence needs.
misconceptions: Functional style is automatically faster and shorter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose functional, imperative, or mixed structure from change risk and evidence needs.

competencies.statement: Write deterministic pure functions whose outputs and failures depend only on explicit inputs.
misconceptions: Avoiding global variables alone guarantees purity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write deterministic pure functions whose outputs and failures depend only on explicit inputs.

competencies.statement: Predict mutation through shared aliases in nested Python containers.
misconceptions: A new outer list also copies every nested value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict mutation through shared aliases in nested Python containers.

competencies.statement: Return updated immutable records while preserving unchanged data and input values.
misconceptions: Immutability requires copying the entire object graph every time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return updated immutable records while preserving unchanged data and input values.

competencies.statement: Select shallow copy, deep copy, immutable representation, or ownership transfer deliberately.
misconceptions: Deep copying is always safest and cheapest.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select shallow copy, deep copy, immutable representation, or ownership transfer deliberately.

competencies.statement: Test determinism, input preservation, and order independence with changed data.
misconceptions: One repeated example proves absence of side effects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test determinism, input preservation, and order independence with changed data.

competencies.statement: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.
misconceptions: First-class functions execute as soon as referenced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.

competencies.statement: Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.
misconceptions: Callable without details communicates every signature.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.

competencies.statement: Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.
misconceptions: Any two functions compose regardless of their domains.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.

competencies.statement: Create small adapters when neighboring transformation signatures do not align.
misconceptions: Adapters should silently discard mismatched data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create small adapters when neighboring transformation signatures do not align.

competencies.statement: Test identity and associativity expectations where a composition abstraction claims them.
misconceptions: Composition laws hold automatically for effectful functions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test identity and associativity expectations where a composition abstraction claims them.

competencies.statement: Use mapping for one-output-per-input transformations without smuggling filtering effects.
misconceptions: Map may freely change collection length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use mapping for one-output-per-input transformations without smuggling filtering effects.

competencies.statement: Design total predicates that retain records for explicit, testable reasons.
misconceptions: Filter transforms values while preserving all inputs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design total predicates that retain records for explicit, testable reasons.

competencies.statement: Define an accumulator identity and invariant before implementing a fold.
misconceptions: Reduce can infer a safe initial value for every operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define an accumulator identity and invariant before implementing a fold.

competencies.statement: Choose comprehensions, generator expressions, or functional calls by clarity and evaluation needs.
misconceptions: Functional syntax is always more functional than a comprehension.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose comprehensions, generator expressions, or functional calls by clarity and evaluation needs.

competencies.statement: Combine adjacent transformations only when semantics, readability, and failure evidence remain intact.
misconceptions: Fewer passes always produce meaningfully faster code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine adjacent transformations only when semantics, readability, and failure evidence remain intact.

competencies.statement: Trace iter, next, StopIteration, and single-pass iterator state exactly.
misconceptions: Iterable and iterator mean the same reusable object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace iter, next, StopIteration, and single-pass iterator state exactly.

competencies.statement: Explain generator suspension, local state retention, send points, and termination.
misconceptions: A generator computes and stores every result at creation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain generator suspension, local state retention, send points, and termination.

competencies.statement: Measure memory and latency tradeoffs between eager and lazy transformation.
misconceptions: Lazy evaluation is always faster and lower risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure memory and latency tradeoffs between eager and lazy transformation.

competencies.statement: Prevent accidental exhaustion, duplicate consumption, and hidden ordering dependencies.
misconceptions: An iterator restarts automatically in each loop.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent accidental exhaustion, duplicate consumption, and hidden ordering dependencies.

competencies.statement: Close generators and preserve cleanup behavior during early exit and exceptions.
misconceptions: Leaving a lazy pipeline early cannot retain resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close generators and preserve cleanup behavior during early exit and exceptions.

competencies.statement: Combine chain, repeat, cycle, count, and islice with explicit termination.
misconceptions: Every itertools function eventually stops on its own.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine chain, repeat, cycle, count, and islice with explicit termination.

competencies.statement: Sort or otherwise establish adjacency before using groupby for complete groups.
misconceptions: Groupby gathers equal values from anywhere in a stream.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sort or otherwise establish adjacency before using groupby for complete groups.

competencies.statement: Estimate product, permutation, and combination growth before enumeration.
misconceptions: Lazy combinatorics remove exponential runtime cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate product, permutation, and combination growth before enumeration.

competencies.statement: Implement pairwise, batched, accumulated, and sliding-window transformations with edge cases.
misconceptions: All window operations handle short streams identically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement pairwise, batched, accumulated, and sliding-window transformations with edge cases.

competencies.statement: Attach explicit limits, backpressure decisions, and cancellation to open-ended iterators.
misconceptions: Calling next repeatedly is sufficient resource control.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attach explicit limits, backpressure decisions, and cancellation to open-ended iterators.

competencies.statement: Use partial application to bind stable configuration without hiding required arguments.
misconceptions: Partial evaluates the function immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use partial application to bind stable configuration without hiding required arguments.

competencies.statement: Use cache and lru_cache only for hashable inputs with stable effect-free semantics.
misconceptions: Caching any function preserves correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use cache and lru_cache only for hashable inputs with stable effect-free semantics.

competencies.statement: Design singledispatch registrations around first-argument type contracts and safe fallbacks.
misconceptions: Single dispatch chooses an implementation from every argument type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design singledispatch registrations around first-argument type contracts and safe fallbacks.

competencies.statement: Use operator callables when they make key extraction and composition more inspectable.
misconceptions: Operator functions avoid all runtime attribute errors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use operator callables when they make key extraction and composition more inspectable.

competencies.statement: Preserve wrapped function identity, signature, and documentation with update_wrapper or wraps.
misconceptions: A wrapper automatically retains metadata.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve wrapped function identity, signature, and documentation with update_wrapper or wraps.

competencies.statement: Trace lexical name capture and cell lookup after the enclosing call returns.
misconceptions: Closures copy every captured value at definition time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace lexical name capture and cell lookup after the enclosing call returns.

competencies.statement: Detect and repair late-bound loop variables in generated callables.
misconceptions: Default arguments and closures always bind at the same time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect and repair late-bound loop variables in generated callables.

competencies.statement: Distinguish deliberate nonlocal state from pure closure configuration and document the effect.
misconceptions: Using nonlocal leaves a function pure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish deliberate nonlocal state from pure closure configuration and document the effect.

competencies.statement: Distinguish currying from partial application and choose only when APIs become clearer.
misconceptions: Currying and partial application are identical operations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish currying from partial application and choose only when APIs become clearer.

competencies.statement: Test factory isolation, captured configuration, invalid inputs, and repeated invocation.
misconceptions: Testing one produced function proves every factory instance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test factory isolation, captured configuration, invalid inputs, and repeated invocation.

competencies.statement: Translate decorator syntax into explicit rebinding and predict application order.
misconceptions: Decorators run only when the decorated function is called.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate decorator syntax into explicit rebinding and predict application order.

competencies.statement: Separate decorator configuration, function wrapping, and call execution across closure layers.
misconceptions: A parameterized decorator needs only one callable layer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate decorator configuration, function wrapping, and call execution across closure layers.

competencies.statement: Preserve signatures, metadata, return values, exceptions, and async behavior through wrappers.
misconceptions: Using wraps preserves all behavioral contracts automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve signatures, metadata, return values, exceptions, and async behavior through wrappers.

competencies.statement: Test how stacked decorators change control flow, data, and failure handling.
misconceptions: Decorator order is cosmetic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test how stacked decorators change control flow, data, and failure handling.

competencies.statement: Reject decorators that hide material domain behavior or make dependencies untestable.
misconceptions: Every repeated line belongs in a decorator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject decorators that hide material domain behavior or make dependencies untestable.

competencies.statement: Define outputs for every admitted input or narrow the domain with an explicit validation boundary.
misconceptions: A function is total when common inputs work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define outputs for every admitted input or narrow the domain with an explicit validation boundary.

competencies.statement: Represent success and failure as explicit immutable variants with useful evidence.
misconceptions: Returning None communicates every failure reason.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent success and failure as explicit immutable variants with useful evidence.

competencies.statement: Use structural pattern matching with guarded, ordered, and intentionally exhaustive cases.
misconceptions: Match automatically warns about every missing case.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use structural pattern matching with guarded, ordered, and intentionally exhaustive cases.

competencies.statement: Choose fail-fast or error accumulation from stakeholder correction needs.
misconceptions: Fail-fast is always simpler for users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose fail-fast or error accumulation from stakeholder correction needs.

competencies.statement: Convert expected domain failures to values while preserving unexpected exceptions and tracebacks.
misconceptions: Functional code must never raise an exception.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert expected domain failures to values while preserving unexpected exceptions and tracebacks.

competencies.statement: Separate concrete examples from general properties and generate boundary-rich cases.
misconceptions: Many random examples constitute a proof.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate concrete examples from general properties and generate boundary-rich cases.

competencies.statement: Test identity, associativity, idempotence, or round-trip laws only where contracts claim them.
misconceptions: Every transformation should satisfy every algebraic law.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test identity, associativity, idempotence, or round-trip laws only where contracts claim them.

competencies.statement: Attach diagnostic taps at effect boundaries without changing transformed values.
misconceptions: Logging inside every pure function keeps it pure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attach diagnostic taps at effect boundaries without changing transformed values.

competencies.statement: Reduce a failing input to the smallest case that still violates the property.
misconceptions: The first large random failure is the best explanation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reduce a failing input to the smallest case that still violates the property.

competencies.statement: Measure bounded consumption and scaling without turning timing noise into correctness claims.
misconceptions: A single fast run proves complexity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure bounded consumption and scaling without turning timing noise into correctness claims.

competencies.statement: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.
misconceptions: A functional core means the whole application has no I/O.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.

competencies.statement: Pass clocks, randomness, storage, and transport behavior through narrow callable interfaces.
misconceptions: Dependency injection requires a class framework.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pass clocks, randomness, storage, and transport behavior through narrow callable interfaces.

competencies.statement: Compose ingestion, validation, transformation, aggregation, and emission with bounded resource use.
misconceptions: A generator pipeline alone supplies backpressure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose ingestion, validation, transformation, aggregation, and emission with bounded resource use.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate behavior preservation with characterization tests and changed-case comparisons.
misconceptions: Cleaner-looking code proves a safe refactor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate behavior preservation with characterization tests and changed-case comparisons.

competencies.statement: Defend functional and imperative boundaries using correctness, operability, and team-maintenance evidence.
misconceptions: Paradigm purity is more important than stakeholder outcomes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend functional and imperative boundaries using correctness, operability, and team-maintenance evidence.

credential.title: Functional Programming in Modern Python Certificate

modules.title: Functional Models, Values, and Observable Effects
modules.description: Refactor a mutable billing script whose hidden state makes reruns disagree. Learners cumulatively produce an effect map and behavior-preserving functional baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish value transformations from identity-changing commands in an execution trace.
Locate I/O, mutation, time, randomness, and exceptions and move them behind explicit boundaries.
Test whether an expression can be replaced by its value without changing program behavior.

modules.title: Pure Functions, Immutability, and Data Copies
modules.description: Repair a pricing pipeline where aliases mutate shared orders during validation. Learners cumulatively produce a pure price transformation with alias-safety tests through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Write deterministic pure functions whose outputs and failures depend only on explicit inputs.
Predict mutation through shared aliases in nested Python containers.
Return updated immutable records while preserving unchanged data and input values.

modules.title: Functions as Values, Signatures, and Composition
modules.description: Turn repeated validation branches into reusable transformations for an intake service. Learners cumulatively produce a typed composition library with signature evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.
Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.
Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.

modules.title: Higher-Order Operations, Map, Filter, and Fold
modules.description: Summarize a stream of service records without hand-written accumulator loops. Learners cumulatively produce a tested map-filter-fold analytics pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use mapping for one-output-per-input transformations without smuggling filtering effects.
Design total predicates that retain records for explicit, testable reasons.
Define an accumulator identity and invariant before implementing a fold.

modules.title: Iteration Protocols, Generators, and Lazy Evaluation
modules.description: Process records larger than memory while exposing malformed rows at a controlled boundary. Learners cumulatively produce a bounded-memory lazy ingestion pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace iter, next, StopIteration, and single-pass iterator state exactly.
Explain generator suspension, local state retention, send points, and termination.
Measure memory and latency tradeoffs between eager and lazy transformation.

modules.title: Itertools, Infinite Streams, and Bounded Pipelines
modules.description: Create schedule combinations and rolling windows without materializing an unbounded search space. Learners cumulatively produce a lazy scheduling and windowing toolkit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Combine chain, repeat, cycle, count, and islice with explicit termination.
Sort or otherwise establish adjacency before using groupby for complete groups.
Estimate product, permutation, and combination growth before enumeration.

modules.title: Functools, Operator Functions, Caching, and Dispatch
modules.description: Build reusable scoring functions without obscuring cache validity or dispatch behavior. Learners cumulatively produce a measured callable utility and dispatch package through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use partial application to bind stable configuration without hiding required arguments.
Use cache and lru_cache only for hashable inputs with stable effect-free semantics.
Design singledispatch registrations around first-argument type contracts and safe fallbacks.

modules.title: Closures, Lexical State, Currying, and Factories
modules.description: Create configurable validators without mutable module globals or late-binding bugs. Learners cumulatively produce a closure-based validator factory with capture tests through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace lexical name capture and cell lookup after the enclosing call returns.
Detect and repair late-bound loop variables in generated callables.
Distinguish deliberate nonlocal state from pure closure configuration and document the effect.

modules.title: Decorators, Cross-Cutting Behavior, and Transparent Wrappers
modules.description: Add retries, timing, authorization checks, and telemetry without changing business function contracts. Learners cumulatively produce a transparent decorator stack with order tests through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate decorator syntax into explicit rebinding and predict application order.
Separate decorator configuration, function wrapping, and call execution across closure layers.
Preserve signatures, metadata, return values, exceptions, and async behavior through wrappers.

modules.title: Total Functions, Result Values, and Pattern Matching
modules.description: Make a batch import explain every rejected record rather than aborting or returning ambiguous sentinels. Learners cumulatively produce a typed parse-validate-result pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define outputs for every admitted input or narrow the domain with an explicit validation boundary.
Represent success and failure as explicit immutable variants with useful evidence.
Use structural pattern matching with guarded, ordered, and intentionally exhaustive cases.

modules.title: Property Tests, Algebraic Laws, and Pipeline Diagnostics
modules.description: Prove a normalization and aggregation library across generated changed cases and injected faults. Learners cumulatively produce a law-oriented regression and diagnostics suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate concrete examples from general properties and generate boundary-rich cases.
Test identity, associativity, idempotence, or round-trip laws only where contracts claim them.
Attach diagnostic taps at effect boundaries without changing transformed values.

modules.title: Functional Core, Imperative Shell, and Production Integration
modules.description: Rebuild an unreliable data-processing service as a pure decision core with explicit operational effects. Learners cumulatively produce a production-shaped functional service and decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.
Pass clocks, randomness, storage, and transport behavior through narrow callable interfaces.
Compose ingestion, validation, transformation, aggregation, and emission with bounded resource use.
